mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is useful for maintaining security compliance and ensuring that your applications are safe from known vulnerabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for regular updates to the vulnerability database and potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official repository for Trivy Operator.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and the version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator in the trivy-system namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for the Trivy Operator to scan images.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: example-deployment
EOF

```

> Creates a sample VulnerabilityReport CR.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: example-deployment
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is processed by the Trivy Operator.

```bash

kubectl get vulnerabilityreport example-vulnerability-report -n default

```

> Verifies the status of the VulnerabilityReport.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all vulnerability reports and any related data.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up current configurations.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create resource: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Custom Resource definition (CRD) is not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/main/deploy/crds/trivy.aquasecurity.github.io_vulnerabilityreports.yaml'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Trivy Operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates a misconfiguration or an issue with the underlying container image.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using: 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' and correct any configuration errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if there are no deployments or resources to scan.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have deployed applications in the cluster that the Trivy Operator can scan.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with your kubeconfig or cluster connectivity.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig file and ensure you can connect to the cluster using: 'kubectl cluster-info'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vulnerability scanning features and improved performance of existing scans.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. The output of 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' should show all pods in a healthy state.

apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: example-deployment
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
